Final Project Markdown

2025-12-08

#Libraries

library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.6
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.1     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.2.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(forecast)
## Registered S3 method overwritten by 'quantmod':
##   method            from
##   as.zoo.data.frame zoo
library(tseries)
library(rugarch)
## Loading required package: parallel
## 
## Attaching package: 'rugarch'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:purrr':
## 
##     reduce
library(TSA)
## Registered S3 methods overwritten by 'TSA':
##   method       from    
##   fitted.Arima forecast
##   plot.Arima   forecast
## 
## Attaching package: 'TSA'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:readr':
## 
##     spec
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     acf, arima
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:utils':
## 
##     tar
library(tsDyn)
library(arfima)
## Loading required package: ltsa
## Note that the arfima package has new defaults starting with
## 1.4-0: type arfimachanges() for a list, as well as some other notes.
## NOTE: some of these are quite important!
## 
## Attaching package: 'arfima'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:forecast':
## 
##     arfima
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     BIC
library(vars)
## Loading required package: MASS
## 
## Attaching package: 'MASS'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     select
## 
## Loading required package: strucchange
## Loading required package: zoo

## Attaching package: 'zoo'

## The following objects are masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.Date, as.Date.numeric
## 
## Loading required package: sandwich

## The following object is masked from 'package:stringr':
##

## 
## Loading required package: urca
## Loading required package: lmtest
library(FinTS)
## 
## Attaching package: 'FinTS'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:forecast':
## 
##     Acf
library(lmtest)
library(splines)
library(mgcv)
## Loading required package: nlme
## 
## Attaching package: 'nlme'
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:forecast':
## 
##     getResponse
## 
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     collapse
## 
## This is mgcv 1.9-3. For overview type 'help("mgcv-package")'.
library(glmnet)  
## Loading required package: Matrix
## 
## Attaching package: 'Matrix'
## 
## The following objects are masked from 'package:tidyr':
## 
##     expand, pack, unpack
## 
## Loaded glmnet 4.1-10

1. IMPORT

bryson_raw <- read_csv("ASA All PGA Raw Data - Tourn Level.csv")
## Rows: 36864 Columns: 37
## ── Column specification ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
## Delimiter: ","
## chr   (5): Player_initial_last, player, tournament name, course, Finish
## dbl  (28): tournament id, player id, hole_par, strokes, hole_DKP, hole_FDP, ...
## lgl   (3): Unnamed: 2, Unnamed: 3, Unnamed: 4
## date  (1): date
## 
## ℹ Use `spec()` to retrieve the full column specification for this data.
## ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.
bryson_clean <- bryson_raw %>%
filter(player == "Bryson DeChambeau") %>%
filter(!is.na(pos)) %>%
mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
arrange(date)

bryson_tourn <- bryson_clean %>%
  group_by(`tournament id`) %>%
  summarise(
    date    = first(date),
    pos     = first(pos),
    sg_putt = mean(sg_putt, na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_ott  = mean(sg_ott,  na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_app  = mean(sg_app,  na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_arg  = mean(sg_arg,  na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_total = mean(sg_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(date)

head(bryson_tourn)
## # A tibble: 6 × 8
##   `tournament id` date         pos sg_putt sg_ott sg_app sg_arg sg_total
##             <dbl> <date>     <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1            2250 2015-06-14    45    0.31   0.29   0.72  -0.51     0.81
## 2            2726 2016-03-16     2  NaN    NaN    NaN    NaN      NaN   
## 3            2493 2016-04-10    21  NaN    NaN    NaN    NaN      NaN   
## 4            2494 2016-04-17     4   -0.01   0.25   1.38   1.02     2.65
## 5            2499 2016-06-05    38   -0.74   0.12  -0.02   1.43     0.79
## 6            2501 2016-06-19    15  NaN    NaN    NaN    NaN      NaN

2. BOX-COX TRANSFORMATION

# Remove tournaments with missing SG putting
sg_putt_clean <- bryson_tourn %>%
  drop_na(sg_putt) %>%
  pull(sg_putt)

# Convert to ts
sg_putt_ts <- ts(sg_putt_clean, frequency = 1)

sg_pos <- sg_putt_ts - min(sg_putt_ts) + 1

lambda <- BoxCox.lambda(sg_pos)
lambda
## [1] 0.7004776
sg_putt_trans <- BoxCox(sg_pos, lambda)
sg_putt_ts_trans <- ts(sg_putt_trans, frequency = 1)

tsdisplay(sg_putt_ts_trans, main = "Transformed SG Putting (Tournament-Level)")

3. STATIONARITY TESTS

adf.test(sg_putt_ts_trans)
## 
##  Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test
## 
## data:  sg_putt_ts_trans
## Dickey-Fuller = -3.9249, Lag order = 4, p-value = 0.01781
## alternative hypothesis: stationary
kpss.test(sg_putt_ts_trans)
## 
##  KPSS Test for Level Stationarity
## 
## data:  sg_putt_ts_trans
## KPSS Level = 0.4702, Truncation lag parameter = 3, p-value = 0.04838

4. DIFFERENCING

sg_diff1 <- diff(sg_putt_ts_trans)
tsdisplay(sg_diff1, main = "First Difference of SG Putting")

adf.test(sg_diff1)
## Warning in adf.test(sg_diff1): p-value smaller than printed p-value
## 
##  Augmented Dickey-Fuller Test
## 
## data:  sg_diff1
## Dickey-Fuller = -5.8407, Lag order = 4, p-value = 0.01
## alternative hypothesis: stationary
kpss.test(sg_diff1)
## Warning in kpss.test(sg_diff1): p-value greater than printed p-value
## 
##  KPSS Test for Level Stationarity
## 
## data:  sg_diff1
## KPSS Level = 0.036888, Truncation lag parameter = 3, p-value = 0.1
returns_ts <- na.omit(ts(as.numeric(sg_diff1)))

5. BASIC FORECAST MODELS

naive_fc <- naive(sg_diff1, h = 5)
mean_fc  <- meanf(sg_diff1, h = 5)
drift_fc <- rwf(sg_putt_ts_trans, drift = TRUE, h = 5)

naive_model <- Arima(sg_diff1, order = c(0,0,0), include.mean = FALSE)
mean_model  <- Arima(sg_diff1, order = c(0,0,0), include.mean = TRUE)
drift_model <- Arima(sg_putt_ts_trans, order = c(0,0,0), include.drift = TRUE)

data.frame(
  Model = c("Naive", "Mean", "Drift"),
  AIC = c(
    AIC(naive_model),
    AIC(mean_model),
    AIC(drift_model)
  )
)
##   Model      AIC
## 1 Naive 200.4445
## 2  Mean 202.4422
## 3 Drift 146.6062

6. EXPONENTIAL SMOOTHING

ets_model <- ets(sg_putt_ts_trans)
ets_fc <- forecast(ets_model, h = 5)
autoplot(ets_fc)

7. SARIMA MODEL

sarima_model <- auto.arima(
sg_diff1,
seasonal = FALSE,
stepwise = FALSE,
approximation = FALSE
)
summary(sarima_model)
## Series: sg_diff1 
## ARIMA(0,0,1) with zero mean 
## 
## Coefficients:
##           ma1
##       -0.9427
## s.e.   0.0427
## 
## sigma^2 = 0.4183:  log likelihood = -73.35
## AIC=150.7   AICc=150.87   BIC=155.31
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                      ME      RMSE       MAE       MPE     MAPE      MASE
## Training set 0.09777299 0.6424094 0.5304461 -12.37601 211.2533 0.4030001
##                    ACF1
## Training set -0.1295526
checkresiduals(sarima_model)

## 
##  Ljung-Box test
## 
## data:  Residuals from ARIMA(0,0,1) with zero mean
## Q* = 7.2012, df = 9, p-value = 0.6162
## 
## Model df: 1.   Total lags used: 10

8. ARCH GARCH

tsdisplay(returns_ts, main = "SG Putting Returns")

ArchTest(returns_ts, lags = 10)
## 
##  ARCH LM-test; Null hypothesis: no ARCH effects
## 
## data:  returns_ts
## Chi-squared = 18.452, df = 10, p-value = 0.04781
garch11_spec <- ugarchspec(
variance.model=list(model="sGARCH", garchOrder=c(1,1)),
mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(0,0)),
distribution.model="norm"
)
garch11_fit <- ugarchfit(spec=garch11_spec, data=returns_ts)
## Warning in .sgarchfit(spec = spec, data = data, out.sample = out.sample, : 
## ugarchfit-->waring: using less than 100 data
##  points for estimation
# t-distribution

garch11_t_spec <- ugarchspec(
variance.model=list(model="sGARCH", garchOrder=c(1,1)),
mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(0,0)),
distribution.model="std"
)
garch11_t_fit <- ugarchfit(spec=garch11_t_spec, data=returns_ts)
## Warning in .sgarchfit(spec = spec, data = data, out.sample = out.sample, : 
## ugarchfit-->waring: using less than 100 data
##  points for estimation
# EGARCH

egarch11_spec <- ugarchspec(
variance.model=list(model="eGARCH", garchOrder=c(1,1)),
mean.model=list(armaOrder=c(0,0)),
distribution.model="std"
)
egarch11_fit <- ugarchfit(spec=egarch11_spec, data=returns_ts)
## Warning in .egarchfit(spec = spec, data = data, out.sample = out.sample, : 
## ugarchfit-->waring: using less than 100 data
##  points for estimation

9. TAR MODELS

Tsay.test(returns_ts)
## $test.stat
## [1] 0.3796
## 
## $p.value
## [1] 0.9504
## 
## $order
## [1] 4
tar_m2    <- setar(returns_ts, m=2, thDelay=0)
tar_m3    <- setar(returns_ts, m=3, thDelay=0)
## Warning: Possible unit root in the low regime. Roots are: 0.9534 0.9534 2.238
tar_m2_d1 <- setar(returns_ts, m=2, thDelay=1)

10. FARIMA

library(arfima)

farima_model <- arfima(returns_ts)
## Note: only one starting point.  Only one mode can be found -- this is now the default behavior.
## Beginning the fits with 1 starting values.
summary(farima_model)
## 
## Call:
##  
## arfima(z = returns_ts)
## 
## 
## Mode 1 Coefficients:
##                 Estimate   Std. Error Th. Std. Err.      z-value Pr(>|z|)    
## d.f         -9.99275e-01  6.32456e-07   9.06366e-02 -1.57999e+06  < 2e-16 ***
## Fitted mean  6.71226e-03  3.32866e-03            NA  2.01650e+00 0.043747 *  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## sigma^2 estimated as 0.397694; Log-likelihood = 32.9745; AIC = -59.949; BIC = -53.0368
## 
## Numerical Correlations of Coefficients:
##             d.f  Fitted mean
## d.f         1.00 0.00       
## Fitted mean 0.00 1.00       
## 
## Theoretical Correlations of Coefficients:
##     d.f 
## d.f 1.00
## 
## Expected Fisher Information Matrix of Coefficients:
##     d.f 
## d.f 1.64
d_hat <- coef(farima_model)[1]
d_hat
## [1] -0.9992751

11. MODEL COMPARISON BY AIC

aic_table <- tibble(
Model = c("Naive","Mean","Drift","ETS","SARIMA","GARCH11","GARCH11-t","EGARCH","TAR2","TAR3","TAR2d1","FARIMA"),
AIC = c(
AIC(naive_model),
AIC(mean_model),
AIC(drift_model),
AIC(ets_model),
AIC(sarima_model),
infocriteria(garch11_fit)[1],
infocriteria(garch11_t_fit)[1],
infocriteria(egarch11_fit)[1],
AIC(tar_m2),
AIC(tar_m3),
AIC(tar_m2_d1),
AIC(farima_model)
)
)

aic_table %>% arrange(AIC)
## # A tibble: 12 × 2
##    Model        AIC
##    <chr>      <dbl>
##  1 FARIMA    -59.9 
##  2 TAR3      -46.7 
##  3 TAR2      -42.9 
##  4 TAR2d1    -37.6 
##  5 EGARCH      2.73
##  6 GARCH11     2.79
##  7 GARCH11-t   2.82
##  8 Drift     147.  
##  9 SARIMA    151.  
## 10 Naive     200.  
## 11 Mean      202.  
## 12 ETS       262.

12. ARIMAX

min_len <- min(length(sg_diff1), length(bryson_tourn$sg_total))

y <- sg_diff1[1:min_len]
x <- bryson_tourn$sg_total[1:min_len]

arimax_model <- auto.arima(
y,
xreg = x,
stepwise = FALSE,
approximation = FALSE
)

summary(arimax_model)
## Series: y 
## Regression with ARIMA(4,0,0) errors 
## 
## Coefficients:
##           ar1      ar2      ar3      ar4    xreg
##       -0.7515  -0.3917  -0.4843  -0.2788  0.0106
## s.e.   0.1335   0.1617   0.1505   0.1305  0.0244
## 
## sigma^2 = 0.375:  log likelihood = -64.22
## AIC=140.43   AICc=141.69   BIC=154.26
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                      ME      RMSE       MAE       MPE     MAPE      MASE
## Training set 0.04609559 0.6567168 0.5367246 -33.72607 250.8583 0.4077701
##                     ACF1
## Training set -0.03868529
coeftest(arimax_model)
## 
## z test of coefficients:
## 
##       Estimate Std. Error z value  Pr(>|z|)    
## ar1  -0.751527   0.133525 -5.6284 1.819e-08 ***
## ar2  -0.391702   0.161686 -2.4226  0.015409 *  
## ar3  -0.484322   0.150531 -3.2174  0.001294 ** 
## ar4  -0.278775   0.130548 -2.1354  0.032727 *  
## xreg  0.010573   0.024350  0.4342  0.664129    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

ARIMAX (SG PUTTING IN LEVELS, NO MANUAL DIFFERENCING)

min_len <- min(length(bryson_tourn$sg_putt),
               length(bryson_tourn$sg_total))

y <- bryson_tourn$sg_putt[1:min_len]
x <- bryson_tourn$sg_total[1:min_len]

arimax_levels <- auto.arima(
  y,
  xreg = x,
  stepwise = FALSE,
  approximation = FALSE
)

summary(arimax_levels)
## Series: y 
## Regression with ARIMA(0,0,0) errors 
## 
## Coefficients:
##         xreg
##       0.3145
## s.e.  0.0385
## 
## sigma^2 = 0.5097:  log likelihood = -80.65
## AIC=165.29   AICc=165.43   BIC=170.34
## 
## Training set error measures:
##                        ME      RMSE       MAE  MPE MAPE      MASE       ACF1
## Training set -0.001995643 0.7091706 0.5836347 -Inf  Inf 0.5765017 -0.0296215
coeftest(arimax_levels)
## 
## z test of coefficients:
## 
##      Estimate Std. Error z value  Pr(>|z|)    
## xreg 0.314506   0.038483  8.1727 3.015e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

13. VAR MODEL

var_df <- bryson_tourn %>%
  dplyr::select(pos, sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg) %>%
  drop_na()

var_ts <- ts(var_df)

var_sel <- VARselect(var_ts, lag.max = 6, type = "const")

var_model <- VAR(var_ts, p = var_sel$selection["AIC(n)"], type = "const")
summary(var_model)
## 
## VAR Estimation Results:
## ========================= 
## Endogenous variables: pos, sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg 
## Deterministic variables: const 
## Sample size: 74 
## Log Likelihood: -563.459 
## Roots of the characteristic polynomial:
## 0.4271 0.2187 0.2014 0.2014 0.05585
## Call:
## VAR(y = var_ts, p = var_sel$selection["AIC(n)"], type = "const")
## 
## 
## Estimation results for equation pos: 
## ==================================== 
## pos = pos.l1 + sg_putt.l1 + sg_ott.l1 + sg_app.l1 + sg_arg.l1 + const 
## 
##            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## pos.l1       0.2683     0.2158   1.244   0.2179  
## sg_putt.l1   4.2363     3.3585   1.261   0.2115  
## sg_ott.l1   -1.3945     4.4427  -0.314   0.7546  
## sg_app.l1    0.2757     3.4342   0.080   0.9363  
## sg_arg.l1    8.7624     4.0766   2.149   0.0352 *
## const       13.2104     8.8890   1.486   0.1419  
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## 
## Residual standard error: 17.63 on 68 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-Squared: 0.1184,  Adjusted R-squared: 0.05358 
## F-statistic: 1.827 on 5 and 68 DF,  p-value: 0.1192 
## 
## 
## Estimation results for equation sg_putt: 
## ======================================== 
## sg_putt = pos.l1 + sg_putt.l1 + sg_ott.l1 + sg_app.l1 + sg_arg.l1 + const 
## 
##            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## pos.l1     -0.01058    0.01021  -1.036   0.3037  
## sg_putt.l1 -0.18649    0.15886  -1.174   0.2445  
## sg_ott.l1   0.11177    0.21014   0.532   0.5965  
## sg_app.l1   0.04992    0.16244   0.307   0.7595  
## sg_arg.l1  -0.34449    0.19282  -1.787   0.0785 .
## const       0.72107    0.42046   1.715   0.0909 .
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.8338 on 68 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-Squared: 0.1176,  Adjusted R-squared: 0.05271 
## F-statistic: 1.812 on 5 and 68 DF,  p-value: 0.122 
## 
## 
## Estimation results for equation sg_ott: 
## ======================================= 
## sg_ott = pos.l1 + sg_putt.l1 + sg_ott.l1 + sg_app.l1 + sg_arg.l1 + const 
## 
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)   
## pos.l1     -0.009705   0.007033  -1.380  0.17215   
## sg_putt.l1 -0.049728   0.109474  -0.454  0.65110   
## sg_ott.l1   0.189712   0.144814   1.310  0.19459   
## sg_app.l1  -0.250096   0.111942  -2.234  0.02876 * 
## sg_arg.l1  -0.028878   0.132879  -0.217  0.82860   
## const       0.888943   0.289745   3.068  0.00309 **
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.5746 on 68 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-Squared: 0.1316,  Adjusted R-squared: 0.06777 
## F-statistic: 2.061 on 5 and 68 DF,  p-value: 0.08097 
## 
## 
## Estimation results for equation sg_app: 
## ======================================= 
## sg_app = pos.l1 + sg_putt.l1 + sg_ott.l1 + sg_app.l1 + sg_arg.l1 + const 
## 
##            Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)  
## pos.l1     -0.01277    0.01019  -1.252   0.2148  
## sg_putt.l1 -0.30039    0.15868  -1.893   0.0626 .
## sg_ott.l1  -0.27924    0.20991  -1.330   0.1879  
## sg_app.l1  -0.09867    0.16226  -0.608   0.5451  
## sg_arg.l1  -0.28490    0.19261  -1.479   0.1437  
## const       0.97414    0.41998   2.319   0.0234 *
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.8329 on 68 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-Squared: 0.06562, Adjusted R-squared: -0.003084 
## F-statistic: 0.9551 on 5 and 68 DF,  p-value: 0.4515 
## 
## 
## Estimation results for equation sg_arg: 
## ======================================= 
## sg_arg = pos.l1 + sg_putt.l1 + sg_ott.l1 + sg_app.l1 + sg_arg.l1 + const 
## 
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)
## pos.l1      0.001062   0.007293   0.146    0.885
## sg_putt.l1 -0.127330   0.113528  -1.122    0.266
## sg_ott.l1  -0.162543   0.150176  -1.082    0.283
## sg_app.l1  -0.010351   0.116087  -0.089    0.929
## sg_arg.l1   0.027796   0.137799   0.202    0.841
## const       0.200177   0.300474   0.666    0.508
## 
## 
## Residual standard error: 0.5959 on 68 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-Squared: 0.08127, Adjusted R-squared: 0.01372 
## F-statistic: 1.203 on 5 and 68 DF,  p-value: 0.3171 
## 
## 
## 
## Covariance matrix of residuals:
##             pos  sg_putt   sg_ott   sg_app   sg_arg
## pos     310.732 -5.78556 -4.79303 -8.63481 -2.23937
## sg_putt  -5.786  0.69522  0.02730 -0.04851 -0.04850
## sg_ott   -4.793  0.02730  0.33015  0.13591 -0.07339
## sg_app   -8.635 -0.04851  0.13591  0.69366  0.01836
## sg_arg   -2.239 -0.04850 -0.07339  0.01836  0.35505
## 
## Correlation matrix of residuals:
##             pos  sg_putt   sg_ott   sg_app   sg_arg
## pos      1.0000 -0.39363 -0.47322 -0.58815 -0.21320
## sg_putt -0.3936  1.00000  0.05697 -0.06986 -0.09763
## sg_ott  -0.4732  0.05697  1.00000  0.28401 -0.21435
## sg_app  -0.5881 -0.06986  0.28401  1.00000  0.03700
## sg_arg  -0.2132 -0.09763 -0.21435  0.03700  1.00000
causality(var_model, cause="sg_putt")$Granger
## 
##  Granger causality H0: sg_putt do not Granger-cause pos sg_ott sg_app
##  sg_arg
## 
## data:  VAR object var_model
## F-Test = 1.1865, df1 = 4, df2 = 340, p-value = 0.3165
causality(var_model, cause="sg_app")$Granger
## 
##  Granger causality H0: sg_app do not Granger-cause pos sg_putt sg_ott
##  sg_arg
## 
## data:  VAR object var_model
## F-Test = 1.8662, df1 = 4, df2 = 340, p-value = 0.1159
irf_putt_on_pos <- irf(var_model, impulse="sg_putt", response="pos", n.ahead=10)
plot(irf_putt_on_pos)

14. REGRESSION

reg_df <- bryson_tourn %>%
  dplyr::select(pos, sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total) %>%
  drop_na()

# Linear model
reg1 <- lm(pos ~ sg_putt + sg_ott + sg_app + sg_arg, data = reg_df)
summary(reg1)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = pos ~ sg_putt + sg_ott + sg_app + sg_arg, data = reg_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##     Min      1Q  Median      3Q     Max 
## -29.442  -5.401  -1.188   6.405  21.191 
## 
## Coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   37.615      1.992  18.886  < 2e-16 ***
## sg_putt      -10.263      1.414  -7.259 4.20e-10 ***
## sg_ott       -11.775      2.145  -5.489 6.10e-07 ***
## sg_app       -10.178      1.493  -6.815 2.71e-09 ***
## sg_arg        -8.678      2.077  -4.178 8.36e-05 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 10.24 on 70 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.7012, Adjusted R-squared:  0.6841 
## F-statistic: 41.06 on 4 and 70 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
# Polynomial
reg_poly <- lm(
  pos ~ poly(sg_putt,2) + poly(sg_ott,2) + poly(sg_app,2) + poly(sg_arg,2),
  data = reg_df
)
summary(reg_poly)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = pos ~ poly(sg_putt, 2) + poly(sg_ott, 2) + poly(sg_app, 
##     2) + poly(sg_arg, 2), data = reg_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -29.6374  -4.7165  -0.8861   4.6715  21.4574 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)         20.720      1.143  18.135  < 2e-16 ***
## poly(sg_putt, 2)1  -78.925     10.361  -7.618 1.28e-10 ***
## poly(sg_putt, 2)2    5.663     10.718   0.528   0.5990    
## poly(sg_ott, 2)1   -59.534     11.519  -5.169 2.37e-06 ***
## poly(sg_ott, 2)2    24.438     10.448   2.339   0.0224 *  
## poly(sg_app, 2)1   -68.846     11.191  -6.152 5.04e-08 ***
## poly(sg_app, 2)2     5.834     10.441   0.559   0.5782    
## poly(sg_arg, 2)1   -47.925     10.581  -4.530 2.54e-05 ***
## poly(sg_arg, 2)2    12.953     10.707   1.210   0.2307    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 9.895 on 66 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.737,  Adjusted R-squared:  0.7051 
## F-statistic: 23.11 on 8 and 66 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
# Splines
library(splines)
reg_spline <- lm(
  pos ~ bs(sg_putt,4) + bs(sg_ott,4) + bs(sg_app,4) + bs(sg_arg,4),
  data = reg_df
)
summary(reg_spline)
## 
## Call:
## lm(formula = pos ~ bs(sg_putt, 4) + bs(sg_ott, 4) + bs(sg_app, 
##     4) + bs(sg_arg, 4), data = reg_df)
## 
## Residuals:
##      Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
## -26.4861  -4.6491  -0.6925   4.7651  21.5256 
## 
## Coefficients:
##                 Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)       145.70      23.56   6.185 6.75e-08 ***
## bs(sg_putt, 4)1   -20.01      14.32  -1.397 0.167680    
## bs(sg_putt, 4)2   -22.95      11.39  -2.015 0.048571 *  
## bs(sg_putt, 4)3   -40.78      15.71  -2.596 0.011921 *  
## bs(sg_putt, 4)4   -48.02      14.43  -3.328 0.001522 ** 
## bs(sg_ott, 4)1     -3.06      19.10  -0.160 0.873248    
## bs(sg_ott, 4)2    -54.32      15.31  -3.548 0.000777 ***
## bs(sg_ott, 4)3    -34.56      16.37  -2.111 0.039068 *  
## bs(sg_ott, 4)4    -50.03      14.22  -3.518 0.000853 ***
## bs(sg_app, 4)1    -33.93      24.18  -1.404 0.165794    
## bs(sg_app, 4)2    -17.05      11.13  -1.532 0.130893    
## bs(sg_app, 4)3    -52.56      18.40  -2.856 0.005936 ** 
## bs(sg_app, 4)4    -42.70      14.03  -3.044 0.003502 ** 
## bs(sg_arg, 4)1    -25.57      19.07  -1.340 0.185338    
## bs(sg_arg, 4)2    -21.48      15.71  -1.367 0.177009    
## bs(sg_arg, 4)3    -42.89      13.65  -3.142 0.002643 ** 
## bs(sg_arg, 4)4    -36.40      13.64  -2.669 0.009861 ** 
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Residual standard error: 10.03 on 58 degrees of freedom
## Multiple R-squared:  0.7623, Adjusted R-squared:  0.6967 
## F-statistic: 11.63 on 16 and 58 DF,  p-value: 1.08e-12
# GAM
library(mgcv)
reg_gam <- gam(pos ~ s(sg_putt) + s(sg_ott) + s(sg_app) + s(sg_arg),
               data = reg_df)
summary(reg_gam)
## 
## Family: gaussian 
## Link function: identity 
## 
## Formula:
## pos ~ s(sg_putt) + s(sg_ott) + s(sg_app) + s(sg_arg)
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##             Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
## (Intercept)   20.720      1.132   18.31   <2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##              edf Ref.df     F  p-value    
## s(sg_putt) 1.000  1.000 57.70  < 2e-16 ***
## s(sg_ott)  2.232  2.831 12.45 3.73e-06 ***
## s(sg_app)  1.000  1.000 46.16  < 2e-16 ***
## s(sg_arg)  1.364  1.653 15.20 0.000147 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.711   Deviance explained = 73.3%
## GCV =  105.3  Scale est. = 96.04     n = 75
plot(reg_gam)

library(broom)

reg1_table <- tidy(reg1) %>%
  dplyr::select(term, estimate, std.error, statistic, p.value)

reg1_table
## # A tibble: 5 × 5
##   term        estimate std.error statistic  p.value
##   <chr>          <dbl>     <dbl>     <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1 (Intercept)    37.6       1.99     18.9  3.47e-29
## 2 sg_putt       -10.3       1.41     -7.26 4.20e-10
## 3 sg_ott        -11.8       2.15     -5.49 6.10e- 7
## 4 sg_app        -10.2       1.49     -6.82 2.71e- 9
## 5 sg_arg         -8.68      2.08     -4.18 8.36e- 5
par(mfrow = c(2, 2))

plot(
  reg_gam,
  shade = TRUE,
  shade.col = "lightgray",
  residuals = FALSE,
  seWithMean = TRUE,
  main = ""
)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

#15. K-MEANS CLUSTERING

library(tidyverse)
library(factoextra)
## Welcome! Want to learn more? See two factoextra-related books at https://goo.gl/ve3WBa
library(cluster)
library(NbClust)

# 1. Identify rows with complete SG data
complete_idx <- complete.cases(
  bryson_tourn[, c("sg_putt", "sg_ott", "sg_app", "sg_arg", "sg_total")]
)

# 2. Build clustering matrix only for those rows
cluster_mat <- bryson_tourn[complete_idx, ] %>%
  dplyr::select(sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total) %>%
  scale()

# 3. (Optional) pick k using fviz_nbclust
# fviz_nbclust(cluster_mat, kmeans, method = "wss")
# fviz_nbclust(cluster_mat, kmeans, method = "silhouette")

# 4. Fit k-means
set.seed(123)
kmeans_model <- kmeans(cluster_mat, centers = 3, nstart = 25)

# 5. Visualize clusters (only for complete rows)
fviz_cluster(kmeans_model, data = cluster_mat,
             main = "K-Means Clustering of Bryson SG Profiles")

# 6. Attach cluster labels back to bryson_tourn
bryson_tourn$cluster_kmeans <- NA_integer_       # pre-fill with NA
bryson_tourn$cluster_kmeans[complete_idx] <- kmeans_model$cluster

head(bryson_tourn)
## # A tibble: 6 × 9
##   `tournament id` date         pos sg_putt sg_ott sg_app sg_arg sg_total
##             <dbl> <date>     <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>    <dbl>
## 1            2250 2015-06-14    45    0.31   0.29   0.72  -0.51     0.81
## 2            2726 2016-03-16     2  NaN    NaN    NaN    NaN      NaN   
## 3            2493 2016-04-10    21  NaN    NaN    NaN    NaN      NaN   
## 4            2494 2016-04-17     4   -0.01   0.25   1.38   1.02     2.65
## 5            2499 2016-06-05    38   -0.74   0.12  -0.02   1.43     0.79
## 6            2501 2016-06-19    15  NaN    NaN    NaN    NaN      NaN   
## # ℹ 1 more variable: cluster_kmeans <int>

#16. HIERARCHICAL CLUSTERING

# 16. HIERARCHICAL CLUSTERING (SG PATTERNS)

# If this chunk runs on its own, recreate complete_idx & cluster_mat:
complete_idx <- complete.cases(
  bryson_tourn[, c("sg_putt", "sg_ott", "sg_app", "sg_arg", "sg_total")]
)

cluster_mat <- bryson_tourn[complete_idx, ] %>%
  dplyr::select(sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total) %>%
  scale()

# Distance + hierarchical clustering
dist_matrix <- dist(cluster_mat, method = "euclidean")
hc <- hclust(dist_matrix, method = "ward.D2")

# Plot dendrogram
plot(hc, main = "Hierarchical Clustering of SG Profiles", xlab = "", sub = "")

# Cut into 3 clusters (for example)
hc_clusters <- cutree(hc, k = 3)

# Add to bryson_tourn (only for complete rows)
bryson_tourn$cluster_hc <- NA_integer_
bryson_tourn$cluster_hc[complete_idx] <- hc_clusters

# Compare to k-means on the same complete cases
table(
  KMeans = bryson_tourn$cluster_kmeans[complete_idx],
  HC     = bryson_tourn$cluster_hc[complete_idx]
)
##       HC
## KMeans  1  2  3
##      1 24  0  7
##      2 10  4  3
##      3  3 15  9

17. DTW CLUSTERING OF SG COMPONENTS

library(TSclust)
## Loading required package: pdc
library(dtw)
## Loading required package: proxy
## 
## Attaching package: 'proxy'
## The following object is masked from 'package:Matrix':
## 
##     as.matrix
## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats':
## 
##     as.dist, dist
## The following object is masked from 'package:base':
## 
##     as.matrix
## Loaded dtw v1.23-1. See ?dtw for help, citation("dtw") for use in publication.
# 1. Identify complete rows across ALL SG variables
idx_comp <- complete.cases(
  bryson_tourn[, c("sg_putt", "sg_ott", "sg_app", "sg_arg", "sg_total")]
)

# 2. Build matrix using only complete rows (DTW cannot handle NA)
sg_mat <- bryson_tourn[idx_comp, ] %>%
  dplyr::select(sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total) %>%
  t()   # rows = SG components, cols = time

# 3. Compute DTW distance
dtw_dist <- TSclust::diss(sg_mat, "DTW")

# 4. Hierarchical clustering
hc_dtw <- hclust(as.dist(dtw_dist), method = "average")

plot(hc_dtw, main = "DTW Hierarchical Clustering of SG Components")

# 5. Choose 2 clusters (or test others)
sg_component_clusters <- cutree(hc_dtw, k = 2)
sg_component_clusters
##  sg_putt   sg_ott   sg_app   sg_arg sg_total 
##        1        1        1        1        2

18. SUBSEQUENCE CLUSTERING (SG_TOTAL HOT/COLD STREAKS)

library(dtw)
library(ggplot2)

series <- bryson_tourn$sg_total
series <- series[!is.na(series)]  # drop NAs for subsequences

window <- 5
subseq <- embed(series, window)[, window:1]

subseq_dist <- dist(subseq, method = "DTW")

set.seed(123)
subseq_kmeans <- kmeans(subseq_dist, centers = 3)

df_sub <- data.frame(
  id      = rep(1:nrow(subseq), each = window),
  value   = as.vector(t(subseq)),
  cluster = rep(subseq_kmeans$cluster, each = window)
)

ggplot(df_sub, aes(x = id, y = value, group = id, color = factor(cluster))) +
  geom_line() +
  ggtitle("Subsequence Clustering: SG_total Hot/Cold Streaks")

19. TIME-POINT CLUSTERING

library(factoextra)

# Only use rows where sg_total is not NA
idx_total <- !is.na(bryson_tourn$sg_total)

sg_scaled <- scale(bryson_tourn$sg_total[idx_total, drop = FALSE])

# Choose k via elbow/silhouette if you want:
# fviz_nbclust(as.data.frame(sg_scaled), kmeans, method = "wss")
# fviz_nbclust(as.data.frame(sg_scaled), kmeans, method = "silhouette")

set.seed(123)
tp_kmeans <- kmeans(sg_scaled, centers = 3, nstart = 25)

# Attach clusters back to full data
bryson_tourn$cluster_timepoint <- NA_integer_
bryson_tourn$cluster_timepoint[idx_total] <- tp_kmeans$cluster

# Plot (only points with sg_total)
ggplot(bryson_tourn[idx_total, ],
       aes(x = date, y = sg_total, color = factor(cluster_timepoint))) +
  geom_line(size = 1.1) +
  geom_point() +
  ggtitle("Time-Point Clustering: SG_total Regimes") +
  theme_minimal()
## Warning: Using `size` aesthetic for lines was deprecated in ggplot2 3.4.0.
## ℹ Please use `linewidth` instead.
## This warning is displayed once every 8 hours.
## Call `lifecycle::last_lifecycle_warnings()` to see where this warning was
## generated.

# 20. CROSS-VALIDATION FOR ARIMA FORECASTs

library(forecast)
library(tidyverse)

cv_arima_all <- function(series, name, seasonal = FALSE) {
  library(forecast)

  # Turn into ts (tournament index as time)
  y_ts <- ts(as.numeric(series), frequency = 1)
  n    <- length(y_ts)

  # Forecast horizon
  h <- 5

  # ---------- 1. Simple 80–20 train/test split ----------
  split      <- floor(0.8 * n)
  train_tt   <- window(y_ts, end   = split)
  test_tt    <- window(y_ts, start = split + 1)

  fit_tt     <- auto.arima(train_tt, seasonal = seasonal)
  fc_tt      <- forecast(fit_tt, h = length(test_tt))

  acc_tt     <- forecast::accuracy(fc_tt, test_tt)
  tt_rmse    <- acc_tt["Test set", "RMSE"]
  tt_mape    <- acc_tt["Test set", "MAPE"]

  # ---------- 2. Rolling-origin CV (h-step ahead) ----------
  roll_err <- tsCV(
    y_ts,
    forecastfunction = function(x, h) {
      forecast(auto.arima(x, seasonal = seasonal), h = h)
    },
    h = h
  )
  roll_rmse <- sqrt(mean(roll_err^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  roll_mape <- mean(abs(roll_err / y_ts), na.rm = TRUE) * 100

  # ---------- 3. Expanding-window CV (1-step ahead) ----------
  exp_err <- tsCV(
    y_ts,
    forecastfunction = function(x, h) {
      forecast(auto.arima(x, seasonal = seasonal), h = h)
    },
    h = 1
  )
  exp_rmse <- sqrt(mean(exp_err^2, na.rm = TRUE))
  exp_mape <- mean(abs(exp_err / y_ts), na.rm = TRUE) * 100

  # ---------- 4. Blocked k-fold CV ----------
  k          <- 5
  block_size <- floor(n / k)
  blk_rmse   <- numeric(k)
  blk_mape   <- numeric(k)

  for (i in seq_len(k)) {
    start_i <- (i - 1) * block_size + 1
    end_i   <- if (i == k) n else i * block_size

    train_idx <- setdiff(seq_len(n), start_i:end_i)
    train_blk <- y_ts[train_idx]
    test_blk  <- y_ts[start_i:end_i]

    fit_blk <- auto.arima(train_blk, seasonal = seasonal)
    fc_blk  <- forecast(fit_blk, h = length(test_blk))

    acc_blk      <- forecast::accuracy(fc_blk, test_blk)
    blk_rmse[i]  <- acc_blk["Test set", "RMSE"]
    blk_mape[i]  <- acc_blk["Test set", "MAPE"]
  }

  tibble::tibble(
    series_name = name,
    method      = c("Train-test", "Rolling", "Expanding", "Blocked"),
    RMSE        = c(tt_rmse, roll_rmse, exp_rmse, mean(blk_rmse)),
    MAPE        = c(tt_mape, roll_mape, exp_mape, mean(blk_mape))
  )
}


cv_all <- bind_rows(
  cv_arima_all(bryson_tourn$sg_putt,  "SG Putting"),
  cv_arima_all(bryson_tourn$sg_ott,   "SG Off-the-Tee"),
  cv_arima_all(bryson_tourn$sg_app,   "SG Approach"),
  cv_arima_all(bryson_tourn$sg_arg,   "SG Around-the-Green"),
  cv_arima_all(bryson_tourn$sg_total, "SG Total"),
  cv_arima_all(bryson_tourn$pos,      "Finishing Position")
)

cv_all
## # A tibble: 24 × 4
##    series_name    method      RMSE  MAPE
##    <chr>          <chr>      <dbl> <dbl>
##  1 SG Putting     Train-test 0.937 150. 
##  2 SG Putting     Rolling    0.897 Inf  
##  3 SG Putting     Expanding  0.915 Inf  
##  4 SG Putting     Blocked    0.927 Inf  
##  5 SG Off-the-Tee Train-test 0.486  30.2
##  6 SG Off-the-Tee Rolling    0.611 112. 
##  7 SG Off-the-Tee Expanding  0.589 104. 
##  8 SG Off-the-Tee Blocked    0.732 248. 
##  9 SG Approach    Train-test 0.706 215. 
## 10 SG Approach    Rolling    0.856 274. 
## # ℹ 14 more rows

21. SPECTRAL ANALYSIS OF STROKES-GAINED SERIES

library(tidyverse)

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# 1. CLEAN DATA: drop tournaments with ANY missing SG value
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
sg_df <- bryson_tourn %>%
  dplyr::select(sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total) %>%  # NOTE: dplyr::select
  drop_na()

# Recreate ts objects AFTER dropping tournaments with NA
sg_putt_ts  <- ts(sg_df$sg_putt)
sg_ott_ts   <- ts(sg_df$sg_ott)
sg_app_ts   <- ts(sg_df$sg_app)
sg_arg_ts   <- ts(sg_df$sg_arg)
sg_total_ts <- ts(sg_df$sg_total)

# Confirm no missing values remain
colSums(is.na(sg_df))
##  sg_putt   sg_ott   sg_app   sg_arg sg_total 
##        0        0        0        0        0
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Helper function for spectral analysis of one series
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
spec_summary <- function(x, name, spans = c(3, 3)) {
  # make sure no NA and numeric
  x <- stats::na.omit(as.numeric(x))

  if (length(x) < 10) {
    warning(paste("Series", name, "too short for spectral analysis"))
    return(NULL)
  }

  spec_obj <- spectrum(
    x,
    log   = "no",
    spans = spans,
    main  = paste("Smoothed Spectrum -", name)
  )

  # Top K peaks in spectral density
  K <- min(5, length(spec_obj$spec))
  ord <- order(spec_obj$spec, decreasing = TRUE)
  top_idx <- ord[1:K]

  data.frame(
    series        = name,
    index         = top_idx,
    frequency     = spec_obj$freq[top_idx],        # cycles per tournament
    period_tourns = 1 / spec_obj$freq[top_idx],    # tournaments per cycle
    density       = spec_obj$spec[top_idx]
  )
}

# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Run spectral analysis for each SG component
# ---------------------------------------------------------------
spec_list <- list(
  spec_summary(sg_putt_ts,  "SG Putting"),
  spec_summary(sg_ott_ts,   "SG Off-the-Tee"),
  spec_summary(sg_app_ts,   "SG Approach"),
  spec_summary(sg_arg_ts,   "SG Around Green"),
  spec_summary(sg_total_ts, "SG Total")
)

# Remove any NULLs just in case and bind rows
spec_results <- do.call(rbind, spec_list[!sapply(spec_list, is.null)])

# View table of dominant cycles (across all series)
spec_results
##             series index  frequency period_tourns   density
## 1       SG Putting    32 0.42666667      2.343750 1.4084727
## 2       SG Putting    31 0.41333333      2.419355 1.3092333
## 3       SG Putting    16 0.21333333      4.687500 1.2803957
## 4       SG Putting     9 0.12000000      8.333333 1.2786206
## 5       SG Putting    33 0.44000000      2.272727 1.1220222
## 6   SG Off-the-Tee     3 0.04000000     25.000000 0.4530301
## 7   SG Off-the-Tee     4 0.05333333     18.750000 0.4414092
## 8   SG Off-the-Tee    29 0.38666667      2.586207 0.4226224
## 9   SG Off-the-Tee    28 0.37333333      2.678571 0.4160517
## 10  SG Off-the-Tee     5 0.06666667     15.000000 0.3858792
## 11     SG Approach    37 0.49333333      2.027027 1.5353842
## 12     SG Approach    23 0.30666667      3.260870 1.2526218
## 13     SG Approach     8 0.10666667      9.375000 1.1423870
## 14     SG Approach     7 0.09333333     10.714286 1.1331177
## 15     SG Approach    22 0.29333333      3.409091 1.1287004
## 16 SG Around Green     9 0.12000000      8.333333 0.5709150
## 17 SG Around Green    13 0.17333333      5.769231 0.5063120
## 18 SG Around Green    24 0.32000000      3.125000 0.5027034
## 19 SG Around Green    10 0.13333333      7.500000 0.4788168
## 20 SG Around Green    23 0.30666667      3.260870 0.4755257
## 21        SG Total    23 0.30666667      3.260870 4.3066704
## 22        SG Total    22 0.29333333      3.409091 3.9960542
## 23        SG Total    31 0.41333333      2.419355 3.7955504
## 24        SG Total    28 0.37333333      2.678571 3.5210457
## 25        SG Total    32 0.42666667      2.343750 3.5096747

22. NEURAL NETWORK MODELS FOR BRYSON (SG TOTAL AS EXAMPLE)

library(forecast)
library(Metrics)
## 
## Attaching package: 'Metrics'
## The following object is masked from 'package:tsDyn':
## 
##     mse
## The following object is masked from 'package:forecast':
## 
##     accuracy
library(dplyr)

# Clean tournament-level data
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)   # for drop_na()

bryson_nn <- bryson_tourn %>%
  dplyr::select(sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total, pos) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()

nnar_one <- function(y, series_name, h = 5, p = 4, size = 5) {
  y <- as.numeric(y)
  y_ts <- ts(y, frequency = 1)

  n  <- length(y_ts)
  train_size <- n - h

  train <- window(y_ts, end = train_size)
  test  <- window(y_ts, start = train_size + 1)

  fit <- nnetar(train, p = p, size = size)
  fc  <- forecast(fit, h = length(test))

  data.frame(
    series = series_name,
    RMSE   = rmse(test, fc$mean),
    MAPE   = mape(test, fc$mean),
    h      = length(test)
  )
}

nnar_results <- bind_rows(
  nnar_one(bryson_nn$sg_putt,  "SG Putting"),
  nnar_one(bryson_nn$sg_ott,   "SG Off-the-Tee"),
  nnar_one(bryson_nn$sg_app,   "SG Approach"),
  nnar_one(bryson_nn$sg_arg,   "SG Around-the-Green"),
  nnar_one(bryson_nn$sg_total, "SG Total"),
  nnar_one(bryson_nn$pos,      "Finishing Position")
)

nnar_results
##                series       RMSE      MAPE h
## 1          SG Putting  1.0543333 1.3821017 5
## 2      SG Off-the-Tee  1.0298409 0.4018960 5
## 3         SG Approach  0.9389566 4.0118211 5
## 4 SG Around-the-Green  0.7198648 2.1127674 5
## 5            SG Total  1.3286328 0.6405047 5
## 6  Finishing Position 17.8564599 3.6680372 5

23. HIDDEN MARKOV MODEL: STREAKY PUTTING REGIMES

library(depmixS4)
## Loading required package: nnet
## 
## Attaching package: 'nnet'
## The following object is masked from 'package:mgcv':
## 
##     multinom
## Loading required package: Rsolnp
library(dplyr)

# -----------------------------------------------------------
# 1. Tournament-level SG putting series
# -----------------------------------------------------------
dat <- bryson_tourn %>%
  arrange(date) %>%                 # ensure tournaments are in time order
  filter(!is.na(sg_putt)) %>%        # drop missing SG putting
  dplyr::select(date, sg_putt)       # explicit namespace to avoid masking

nrow(dat)
## [1] 75
summary(dat$sg_putt)
##    Min. 1st Qu.  Median    Mean 3rd Qu.    Max. 
## -1.2200 -0.1600  0.3600  0.4859  1.1050  2.5000
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# 2. Fit 2-state and 3-state Gaussian HMMs
# -----------------------------------------------------------
set.seed(123)

hmm2 <- depmix(
  sg_putt ~ 1,
  family  = gaussian(),
  nstates = 2,
  data    = dat
)
hmm2_fit <- fit(hmm2, verbose = FALSE)
## converged at iteration 146 with logLik: -92.21386
hmm3 <- depmix(
  sg_putt ~ 1,
  family  = gaussian(),
  nstates = 3,
  data    = dat
)
hmm3_fit <- fit(hmm3, verbose = FALSE)
## converged at iteration 207 with logLik: -87.04609
# Compare model fit
markov_aic <- data.frame(
  Model = c("HMM 2-state", "HMM 3-state"),
  AIC   = c(AIC(hmm2_fit), AIC(hmm3_fit))
)
markov_aic
##         Model      AIC
## 1 HMM 2-state 198.4277
## 2 HMM 3-state 202.0922
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# 3. Inspect preferred model (3-state)
# -----------------------------------------------------------
summary(hmm3_fit)
## Initial state probabilities model 
## pr1 pr2 pr3 
##   0   0   1 
## 
## Transition matrix 
##         toS1  toS2  toS3
## fromS1 0.714 0.286 0.000
## fromS2 1.000 0.000 0.000
## fromS3 0.000 0.136 0.864
## 
## Response parameters 
## Resp 1 : gaussian 
##     Re1.(Intercept) Re1.sd
## St1           0.872  0.679
## St2          -0.507  0.398
## St3          -0.112  0.427
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# 4. Transition matrix (robust extraction)
# -----------------------------------------------------------
nstates <- 3
pars3 <- getpars(hmm3_fit)

tm3 <- matrix(
  pars3[(nstates + 1):(nstates + nstates^2)],
  nrow = nstates,
  byrow = TRUE
)

rownames(tm3) <- paste0("From State ", 1:nstates)
colnames(tm3) <- paste0("To State ", 1:nstates)
tm3
##                To State 1   To State 2   To State 3
## From State 1 7.140625e-01 2.859375e-01 6.237942e-33
## From State 2 9.999996e-01 3.790189e-07 5.401677e-51
## From State 3 1.187837e-27 1.356745e-01 8.643255e-01
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# 5. State means, SDs, and most likely state sequence
# -----------------------------------------------------------
post3 <- posterior(hmm3_fit)
## Warning in .local(object, ...): Argument 'type' not specified and will default
## to 'viterbi'. This default may change in future releases of depmixS4. Please
## see ?posterior for alternative options.
dat$state3 <- post3$state

state_means3 <- tapply(dat$sg_putt, dat$state3, mean)
state_sds3   <- tapply(dat$sg_putt, dat$state3, sd)

state_summary3 <- data.frame(
  State        = as.integer(names(state_means3)),
  Mean_SG_Putt = as.numeric(state_means3),
  SD_SG_Putt   = as.numeric(state_sds3)
)
state_summary3
##   State Mean_SG_Putt SD_SG_Putt
## 1     1     0.925600  0.6361785
## 2     2    -0.564375  0.3526465
## 3     3    -0.090000  0.4484139
# -----------------------------------------------------------
# 6. Plot regimes over time
# -----------------------------------------------------------
ord <- order(state_summary3$Mean_SG_Putt)
state_map <- setNames(1:nstates, state_summary3$State[ord])
dat$state3_ordered <- state_map[as.character(dat$state3)]

plot(
  dat$sg_putt,
  col  = dat$state3_ordered,
  pch  = 16,
  xlab = "Tournament (time)",
  ylab = "Strokes Gained: Putting",
  main = "Hidden Markov Regimes for Bryson's SG Putting"
)

legend(
  "topleft",
  legend = paste0(
    "State ", state_summary3$State,
    " (mean = ", sprintf("%.2f", state_summary3$Mean_SG_Putt), ")"
  ),
  col = 1:nstates,
  pch = 16,
  bty = "n"
)

# PLAYER CLUSTERING: WHERE DOES BRYSON FALL?

library(tidyverse)
library(cluster)
library(factoextra)

# ---- FIX: ensure pga_raw exists in the knit session ----
if (!exists("pga_raw")) {
  if (exists("bryson_raw")) {
    pga_raw <- bryson_raw
  } else if (file.exists("ASA All PGA Raw Data - Tourn Level.csv")) {
    pga_raw <- readr::read_csv("ASA All PGA Raw Data - Tourn Level.csv", show_col_types = FALSE)
  } else {
    stop("Missing data: create `pga_raw` (or `bryson_raw`) earlier, or put the CSV in the working directory.")
  }
}

# ---- 1) Tournament-level aggregation ----
pga_tourn <- pga_raw %>%
  dplyr::mutate(date = as.Date(date)) %>%
  dplyr::filter(!is.na(player), !is.na(`tournament id`)) %>%
  dplyr::group_by(player, `tournament id`) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    date     = suppressWarnings(min(date, na.rm = TRUE)),
    pos      = suppressWarnings(first(na.omit(pos))),
    sg_putt  = mean(sg_putt,  na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_ott   = mean(sg_ott,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_app   = mean(sg_app,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_arg   = mean(sg_arg,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_total = mean(sg_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  )

# ---- 2) Player-level profiles (one row per player) ----
min_tourn <- 20

player_profiles <- pga_tourn %>%
  dplyr::group_by(player) %>%
  dplyr::summarise(
    n_tourn  = sum(complete.cases(sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total)),
    sg_putt  = mean(sg_putt,  na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_ott   = mean(sg_ott,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_app   = mean(sg_app,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_arg   = mean(sg_arg,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sg_total = mean(sg_total, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups  = "drop"
  ) %>%
  dplyr::filter(n_tourn >= min_tourn) %>%
  tidyr::drop_na()

# ---- 3) Scale features ----
features <- player_profiles %>%
  dplyr::select(sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total)

X <- scale(features)

# ---- 4) K-means clustering ----
set.seed(123)
k <- 4
km <- kmeans(X, centers = k, nstart = 50)

player_profiles <- player_profiles %>%
  dplyr::mutate(cluster_kmeans = km$cluster)

# ---- 5) Where does Bryson fall? ----
bryson_name <- "Bryson DeChambeau"

bryson_row <- player_profiles %>%
  dplyr::filter(player == bryson_name)

if (nrow(bryson_row) == 0) {

  message(
    "Bryson not found among eligible players. ",
    "Try lowering min_tourn (currently = ", min_tourn, ")."
  )

} else {

  bryson_x <- X[player_profiles$player == bryson_name, , drop = FALSE]
  bryson_cluster <- bryson_row$cluster_kmeans[1]
  centroid <- km$centers[bryson_cluster, , drop = FALSE]

  dist_to_centroid <- sqrt(sum((bryson_x - centroid)^2))

  cat("\n=== BRYSON CLUSTER RESULT ===\n")

  base::print(
    bryson_row %>%
      dplyr::select(
        player, n_tourn,
        sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg, sg_total,
        cluster_kmeans
      )
  )

  cat(
    "\nDistance to assigned cluster centroid (scaled space): ",
    round(dist_to_centroid, 4), "\n\n"
  )
}
## 
## === BRYSON CLUSTER RESULT ===
## # A tibble: 1 × 8
##   player           n_tourn sg_putt sg_ott sg_app  sg_arg sg_total cluster_kmeans
##   <chr>              <int>   <dbl>  <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl>    <dbl>          <int>
## 1 Bryson DeChambe…     104  0.0482  0.627  0.152 -0.0137    0.814              4
## 
## Distance to assigned cluster centroid (scaled space):  1.3943
# ---- 6) Outputs professors like ----

# A) Cluster sizes
base::print(table(player_profiles$cluster_kmeans))
## 
##   1   2   3   4 
##  33 109  98  92
# B) Cluster centers (back on original SG scale)
centers_scaled <- km$centers

centers_unscaled <- sweep(centers_scaled, 2, attr(X, "scaled:scale"), `*`)
centers_unscaled <- sweep(centers_unscaled, 2, attr(X, "scaled:center"), `+`)

centers_unscaled <- as.data.frame(centers_unscaled)
centers_unscaled$cluster <- 1:k

base::print(
  centers_unscaled %>%
    dplyr::select(cluster, everything())
)
##   cluster     sg_putt      sg_ott     sg_app      sg_arg   sg_total
## 1       1 -0.24072619 -0.63579607 -0.6450631 -0.16539056 -1.6805841
## 2       2 -0.28964162  0.02437879 -0.2533068 -0.20672832 -0.7215462
## 3       3  0.01399079 -0.26156595 -0.1965700  0.07425362 -0.3658051
## 4       4 -0.11512374  0.23551092  0.2358297  0.02272542  0.3847141

#AIC

# ---- helpers (robust) ----
safe_AIC <- function(obj) {
  out <- tryCatch(AIC(obj), error = function(e) NA_real_)
  as.numeric(out)
}

safe_uGARCH_AIC <- function(fit) {
  # rugarch uses infocriteria(); first element is typically AIC
  out <- tryCatch(infocriteria(fit)[1], error = function(e) NA_real_)
  as.numeric(out)
}

# ---- collect AICs from every model that supports it ----
aic_all <- dplyr::bind_rows(

  # -----------------------------
  # A) SG PUTTING UNIVARIATE MODELS (from sections 5–11)
  # -----------------------------
  tibble::tibble(
    group = "SG Putting univariate",
    model = c("Naive (Arima 0,0,0 no mean)",
              "Mean (Arima 0,0,0 mean)",
              "Drift (Arima 0,0,0 drift)",
              "ETS",
              "SARIMA (auto.arima on sg_diff1)"),
    AIC = c(
      safe_AIC(naive_model),
      safe_AIC(mean_model),
      safe_AIC(drift_model),
      safe_AIC(ets_model),
      safe_AIC(sarima_model)
    )
  ),

  # GARCH family (rugarch)
  tibble::tibble(
    group = "SG Putting univariate",
    model = c("GARCH(1,1) normal",
              "GARCH(1,1) t",
              "EGARCH(1,1) t"),
    AIC = c(
      safe_uGARCH_AIC(garch11_fit),
      safe_uGARCH_AIC(garch11_t_fit),
      safe_uGARCH_AIC(egarch11_fit)
    )
  ),

  # TAR + FARIMA
  tibble::tibble(
    group = "SG Putting univariate",
    model = c("TAR m=2",
              "TAR m=3",
              "TAR m=2 delay=1",
              "FARIMA (arfima)"),
    AIC = c(
      safe_AIC(tar_m2),
      safe_AIC(tar_m3),
      safe_AIC(tar_m2_d1),
      safe_AIC(farima_model)
    )
  ),

  # -----------------------------
  # B) ARIMAX MODELS
  # -----------------------------
  tibble::tibble(
    group = "ARIMAX",
    model = c("ARIMAX on sg_diff1 with xreg=sg_total",
              "ARIMAX on sg_putt levels with xreg=sg_total"),
    AIC = c(
      safe_AIC(arimax_model),
      safe_AIC(arimax_levels)
    )
  ),

  # -----------------------------
  # C) FINISHING POSITION MODELS (regressions + GAM)
  # -----------------------------
  tibble::tibble(
    group = "Finishing position models",
    model = c("Linear regression: pos ~ sg_putt + sg_ott + sg_app + sg_arg",
              "Polynomial regression (degree 2)",
              "Spline regression (bs df=4)",
              "GAM: pos ~ s(sg_putt)+s(sg_ott)+s(sg_app)+s(sg_arg)"),
    AIC = c(
      safe_AIC(reg1),
      safe_AIC(reg_poly),
      safe_AIC(reg_spline),
      safe_AIC(reg_gam)
    )
  ),

  # -----------------------------
  # D) VAR MODEL (pos + SG components)
  # -----------------------------
  tibble::tibble(
    group = "Finishing position models",
    model = "VAR (pos, sg_putt, sg_ott, sg_app, sg_arg)",
    AIC = safe_AIC(var_model)
  ),

  # -----------------------------
  # E) HMM MODELS (depmixS4)
  # -----------------------------
  tibble::tibble(
    group = "HMM (SG Putting regimes)",
    model = c("HMM 2-state (Gaussian)",
              "HMM 3-state (Gaussian)"),
    AIC = c(
      safe_AIC(hmm2_fit),
      safe_AIC(hmm3_fit)
    )
  )

) %>%
  dplyr::arrange(AIC)

# ---- print the table ----
aic_all
## # A tibble: 21 × 3
##    group                 model                                    AIC
##    <chr>                 <chr>                                  <dbl>
##  1 SG Putting univariate FARIMA (arfima)                       -59.9 
##  2 SG Putting univariate TAR m=3                               -46.7 
##  3 SG Putting univariate TAR m=2                               -42.9 
##  4 SG Putting univariate TAR m=2 delay=1                       -37.6 
##  5 SG Putting univariate EGARCH(1,1) t                           2.73
##  6 SG Putting univariate GARCH(1,1) normal                       2.79
##  7 SG Putting univariate GARCH(1,1) t                            2.82
##  8 ARIMAX                ARIMAX on sg_diff1 with xreg=sg_total 140.  
##  9 SG Putting univariate Drift (Arima 0,0,0 drift)             147.  
## 10 SG Putting univariate SARIMA (auto.arima on sg_diff1)       151.  
## # ℹ 11 more rows
# ---- optional: add a note block for "models that do NOT have AIC" ----
no_aic_models <- tibble::tibble(
  group = c("Clustering", "Clustering", "Clustering", "Neural nets", "CV/Accuracy tools", "Spectral analysis"),
  model = c("K-means clustering (tournament profiles)",
            "Hierarchical clustering (ward.D2)",
            "DTW clustering of SG components",
            "NNAR (nnetar)",
            "tsCV / forecast accuracy comparisons",
            "Spectrum / spectral peaks"),
  note = c("No AIC (not likelihood-based)",
           "No AIC (not likelihood-based)",
           "No AIC (distance-based)",
           "No AIC (different criterion)",
           "Not an AIC model",
           "Not an AIC model")
)

no_aic_models
## # A tibble: 6 × 3
##   group             model                                    note               
##   <chr>             <chr>                                    <chr>              
## 1 Clustering        K-means clustering (tournament profiles) No AIC (not likeli…
## 2 Clustering        Hierarchical clustering (ward.D2)        No AIC (not likeli…
## 3 Clustering        DTW clustering of SG components          No AIC (distance-b…
## 4 Neural nets       NNAR (nnetar)                            No AIC (different …
## 5 CV/Accuracy tools tsCV / forecast accuracy comparisons     Not an AIC model   
## 6 Spectral analysis Spectrum / spectral peaks                Not an AIC model

#Core AIC

safe_AIC <- function(obj) {
  out <- tryCatch(AIC(obj), error = function(e) NA_real_)
  as.numeric(out)
}

models_core <- list(
  ARIMAX_sg_diff1_with_sg_total = arimax_model,
  VAR_pos_and_SG                = var_model,
  Linear_regression_pos_on_SG   = reg1,
  Polynomial_regression_SG2     = reg_poly,
  Spline_regression_SG_bs4      = reg_spline,
  GAM_pos_on_SG                 = reg_gam,
  HMM_2_state_SG_putt           = hmm2_fit,
  HMM_3_state_SG_putt           = hmm3_fit
)

aic_core <- data.frame(
  Model = names(models_core),
  AIC   = sapply(models_core, safe_AIC)
) %>% dplyr::arrange(AIC)

aic_core
##                                                       Model       AIC
## ARIMAX_sg_diff1_with_sg_total ARIMAX_sg_diff1_with_sg_total  140.4346
## HMM_2_state_SG_putt                     HMM_2_state_SG_putt  198.4277
## HMM_3_state_SG_putt                     HMM_3_state_SG_putt  202.0922
## GAM_pos_on_SG                                 GAM_pos_on_SG  563.4867
## Polynomial_regression_SG2         Polynomial_regression_SG2  567.0524
## Linear_regression_pos_on_SG     Linear_regression_pos_on_SG  568.6156
## Spline_regression_SG_bs4           Spline_regression_SG_bs4  575.4537
## VAR_pos_and_SG                               VAR_pos_and_SG 1186.9177
